title: bromopsis riparia subsp. divaricata
intro: Bromopsis riparia subsp. divaricata, also known as Mountain Brome, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and expectorant qualities, which have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing a range of health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Mountain Brome can be prepared as an herbal tea or decoction, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and relieve stress.
